name: Stew of Fennel with Carrots & Pearl Onions
recipeIngredient: 1 ½ cups Defatted reduced-sodium Chicken stock
2 tablespoons Finely chopped fennel fronds or fresh parsley
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the stalks from the fennel bulbs and use a vegetable peeler to remove strings.
2. Cut each bulb into 8 wedges, leaving the core intact.
3. Bring salted water to a boil in a large saucepan. Add fennel and blanch for 5 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon, blot with paper towels, and set aside.
4. In a large cast-iron or non-stick skillet, heat half a tablespoon butter and half a teaspoon olive oil over medium-high heat.
5. Add the remaining fennel and sauté until browned on all sides, about 8 minutes. Transfer to a dish and reserve.
6. Repeat with the remaining butter, oil, and fennel.
7. Add onions to the skillet, sprinkling with sugar. Sauté, shaking the skillet back and forth, for 5-6 minutes until nicely browned.
8. Return the fennel to the skillet. Add carrots and 1 cup chicken stock. Season with salt and pepper.
9. Simmer, covered, until vegetables are tender, about 15-20 minutes, adding more stock as necessary to keep the stew moist.
10. Garnish with chopped fennel fronds or fresh parsley before serving.
